ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title held by the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the employee is based */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "status" TEXT /* Employment status of the employee (e.g., Active, Inactive, Suspended, Pending) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was terminated, if applicable */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee */,
  "benefit_plan_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benefit plan the employee is enrolled in */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title_name" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department associated with the job title */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job title */,
  "required_qualifications" TEXT /* Required qualifications for the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department, if applicable */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  "head_count" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "location_address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the employment status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Description of the employment status */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the status is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee the action was performed on */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., Export to .pdf, Export to .csv, Export to .doc) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was performed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR Administrator, Manager, Employee) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "is_system_permission" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the permission is a system-level permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, DOC) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the report is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_runs" (
  "report_run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report run */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who ran the report */,
  "run_date" TEXT /* Date the report was run */,
  "run_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the report run */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report run (e.g., Success, Failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_runs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_runs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
